subsection.heading: Federal Charter.—
subsection.content: The American National Red Cross (in this chapter, the “corporation”) is a Federally chartered instrumentality of the United States and a body corporate and politic in the District of Columbia.

subsection.heading: Name.—
subsection.content: The name of the corporation is “The American National Red Cross”. The corporation may conduct its business and affairs, and otherwise hold itself out, as the “American Red Cross” in any jurisdiction.

subsection.heading: Perpetual Existence.—
subsection.content: Except as otherwise provided, the corporation has perpetual existence.

html:td.html:p: Jan. 5, 1905, ch. 23, § 2 (1st sentence words before 2d comma), 33 Stat. 600; May 8, 1947, ch. 50, § 3, 61 Stat. 81.

note.p: This section is substituted for the source provisions to eliminate unnecessary and obsolete language.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2007—Subsec. (a). Pub. L. 110–26, § 3(1), inserted “a Federally chartered instrumentality of the United States and” before “a body corporate and politic”.
Subsec. (b). Pub. L. 110–26, § 3(2), inserted at end “The corporation may conduct its business and affairs, and otherwise hold itself out, as the ‘American Red Cross’ in any jurisdiction.”

note.heading: Findings; Sense of Congress
